response_text:
You're right to ask. Here's why:

**Everything reported success while failing:**

| System | Status | Reality |
|--------|--------|---------|
| Session hooks | ✅ Ran | Captured cron prompts, not conversation |
| Subconscious cron | ✅ Ran | Read same Feb 20 file, said "ok" |
| Muscle Memory cron | ✅ Ran | Read same Feb 20 file, said "ok" |
| Git sync | ✅ Ran | Synced broken/empty content |
| Dream Protocol | ❌ Silent fail | Just didn't run |

**No system raised an alarm.** They all said "ok" while doing nothing useful.

The root cause: **no validation that output is meaningful.** If a cron runs and produces empty output, it still reports "ok."

I should have had:
- Validation: "Did we capture real user messages?"
- Cross-check: "Is today's memory different from yesterday?"
- Alert: "Something is wrong — output is empty"

Instead, everything just kept running, reporting success, while I lost two days of memory.

That's on me. I built a system that looks functional but isn't.
